name: Fastest Salsa in the West
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Ripe, red tomatoes, washed - halved
3 To 6 jalapeno peppers, stemmed and seeded
4 Cloves garlic, minced - through a garlic press
2 tbsp Ground chili powder (New Mexican, if possible)
recipeInstructions:
(Makes about 1-1/2 quarts) Garlic salt or salt to taste.
Roast tomato halves in a baking pan at 400 degrees for 20 minutes. You may need two pans. Cool. Drain and discard any clear juices in the bottom of the pan.
In two batches, place cooled tomato halves in a food processor or blender and roughly puree. Do not over-process. Add pieces of jalapeno while processing the second batch, pulsing on and off.
Place tomato-pepper puree in a wide, 4-quart saucepan with garlic, onions, chili powder, cumin, and vinegar. Simmer for 10 minutes, then taste. Add more seasonings and salt if desired. Refrigerate or freeze.
